---
title: "Figure 4—figure supplement 1 — R against IR, all five parameters: the distortion-induced bias is diffuse for R, confined for IR"
author: "Luciano Moffatt"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

<!--
FIRST MOMENT. This is the bias half of the all-parameters R-vs-IR comparison; the distortion half is
supplement 2. They are split because, unlike Figure 4's two moments (which fail at opposite ends of
the interval axis and must be read together), here each moment INDEPENDENTLY makes the same claim:
R's departure is diffuse across the parameters, IR's is confined to two and vanishes.

Measured, fraction of cells whose 95% bootstrap interval EXCLUDES 0:
                 IR      R
    k_on        0.14   0.79
    k_off       0.13   0.57
    i           0.12   0.88
    N_ch        0.13   0.87
    sigma_noise 0.24   0.75

So R carries a bias in every direction; IR's is at the ~0.13 floor everywhere. Conditioning on the
interval does not merely shrink the bias, it removes it from four of five directions.

DATA: battery_sim_G, anchored at theta_sim (NOT the pool files: at the optimum the score vanishes and
the bias is zero by construction). Gaussian family. Standard 4 N_ch x 3 noise grid. No new runs.

TRAP inherited from figure_4: three cells are off the +-0.3 scale and clamped - macro_R, N_ch 10000,
Delta*k_off = 1, in the i and N_ch directions, where the Gaussian Fisher covariance has kappa ~ 1e5,
so a displacement along a near-null direction costs almost no likelihood. SIGN meaningful, MAGNITUDE
not a physical bias. Same open question across figure_4 and all four supplements: grey those cells as
unidentified, or plot bias/SE.

TRAP: the "noise" label is not the physical noise. current_noise = label/1000; the CSV column
noise_in_conductance_tau stores the LABEL, which is the y axis.
-->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
source("figure_4_supp_common.R")
```

```{r data}
kap  <- read_kappa()
bias <- readcell("battery_sim_G") %>%
  filter(component_path == BIAS, param_index %in% PIDX,
         statistic == "value", probit %in% c("mean", "quantile")) %>%
  shape(ci_toward = 0) %>%
  add_unident(min(BIAS_LIN), max(BIAS_LIN), kap)   # grey where clamped AND ill-conditioned
cat("supp1 (bias):", nrow(CELLS), "cells, all five parameters\n")
cat("scale saturation:\n"); sat(bias$conf, min(BIAS_LIN), max(BIAS_LIN), "bias")
cat("greyed (unidentified) cells:", sum(bias$unident), "->",
    paste(unique(with(filter(bias, unident), paste(ALGO_LAB[algo], param, "N_ch", Num_ch))), collapse = "; "), "\n")
```

```{r plot, fig.width = 7, fig.height = 9.9}
nr <- length(PARAM_ORD) * length(ALGOS)      # 10 rows
gB <- mapblock(bias, function(d) d$conf, BIAS_PAL, BIAS_LIN)
g <- gB / colorbar(BIAS_PAL, BIAS_LAB, "bias (log10 units)", BIAS_LIN) +
     plot_layout(heights = c(0.95 * nr, 0.3))
H <- 0.95 * nr + 0.3 + 0.5
g
ggsave("Figure_4_supplement_1.pdf", g, width = 7.0, height = H)
cat("Figure_4_supplement_1.pdf:", nr, "rows, height", round(H, 1), "in\n")
```

```{r caption-numbers}
cat("\n=== supp 1 (bias) caption numbers ===\n")
cat("\nfraction of cells whose interval excludes 0, per parameter and algorithm\n")
print(as.data.frame(bias %>% group_by(param, algo) %>%
  summarise(frac_nonzero = mean(conf != 0), median = median(conf), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(algo = ALGO_LAB[algo]) %>% arrange(match(param, PARAM_ORD), algo)),
  digits = 3, row.names = FALSE)
```
